texts.full_text: reported as an effective anti-inflammatory compound. We previously showed that naringin had antitussive effect on experimentally induced cough in guinea pigs. However, the effects and mechanism of naringin on lipopolysaccharide (LPS)-induced acute lung injury (ALI) in mice are not fully understood. In this study, our aim was to evaluate the anti-inflammatory activities of naringin on LPS-induced ALI in mice and clarify its underlying mechanisms of action. We found that in vivo pretreatment with naringin markedly decreased the lung wet weight to dry weight ratio, and led to significant attenuation of LPS-induced evident lung histopathological changes. Meanwhile, naringin significantly reduced bronchoalveolar lavage fluid (BALF) total cell and neutrophil (PMN) counts after LPS challenge. Furthermore, naringin inhibited myeloperoxidase (MPO:a marker of neutrophil infiltration) and interleukin-1β (IL-1β) production in BALF. Moreover, naringin significantly inhibited LPS-induced nuclear factor-κB (NF-κB) nuclear translocation in lung tissue. In addition, naringin inhibited LPS-induced phosphorylation of inhibitor of nuclear factor-κB (IκB) kinase (IKK) and IκB-α, and nuclear translocation of NF-κB in RAW264.7 cells. Taken together, these results suggested that naringin may be a potential therapeutic agent for ALI, and that naringin may be able to prevent LPS-induced ALI in

texts.full_text: {#Sec1} ============ The Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2015--2020 have encouraged consumers to choose beverages with no added sugars, such as water, in place of sugar-sweetened beverages (SSB) \[[@CR1]\]. Dietary intake surveys in the US have pointed to a continuing decline in SSB consumption \[[@CR2], [@CR3]\], especially among children, teenagers, and young adults \[[@CR3]--[@CR6]\]. However, SSB consumption rates are still relatively high among racial/ethnic minorities and younger age groups and continue to be associated with higher obesity risk \[[@CR3], [@CR4], [@CR6]\]. Replacing SSB with plain drinking water has become central to health promotion strategies, including those led by US federal agencies \[[@CR1]\]. The Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010 \[[@CR7]\] requires schools participating in the National School Lunch Program to offer free drinking water to students. Additionally, the US Department of Agriculture (USDA) has implemented the Smart Snacks in School nutrition standards, which limit the availability of SSB in schools \[[@CR8]\]. These initiatives aim to improve children's access to water and reduce their consumption of

texts.full_text: r talk, or explore with the ArtStart toucThe National Gallery in London is an incredible repository of Western European art spanning from medieval times through Renaissance masters like Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo to Impressionists such as Monet. One cannot help but be captivated by the sheer breadth, depth or beauty on display. A visit here doesn't have a set entry fee; it's open for everyone regardless of their financial situation—a testament not only to its value in cultural heritage preservation (it houses works from artists like Van Gogh and Renoir)but also reflects Britain’s commitment towards democratizing access. The museum offers free audio guides, which are perfect if you prefer a self-guided tour; or guided tours that provide insightful commentary on the exhibits—a great way to learn more about specific pieces of art. In addition there's an impressive array of special exhibitions and events held throughout year-round providing visitors with varied experiences. These range from lectures, film screenings (often featuring works by British artists), concerts as well workshops which are often free or have a nominal charge attached—making the experience accessible to all sectors within society including families. The National Gallery is also home to an impressive library of art books and manuscripts that can be accessed upon request. This resource

Interestingly and to expectations, the most common motivator was not performance-linked incentive such as marks for contribution but(a) the typeof discussion topic followed by (b) the types ofresponses from other participants. Furtheranalyses revealed that more graduate students reported being motivated by personal gain motives compared to undergraduates and working adults, and fewer undergraduates reported beingmotivated by the enjoyment of the discussion compared to graduates and working adults. More undergraduate and graduate studentsreported being motivated by marks compared to working adults. Synthesizing the findings of this study and those reported in other previous studies produces a more updated and comprehensive understanding of what motivates students to contribute inpeer-facilitated online discussions. Implications of the findingsare discussed. Article Preview Top LiteratureReview The roleof an online facilitator is traditionally fulfilled by the instructor. While instructors may play animportant role in facilitating online asynchronous discussion, not all researchers agreethat this might be the best choice. First,not all instructors are dedicate the time and energy to facilitate the discussions (Correia & Baran,2010).Second, a discussionthat is instructor-facilitated may result in discussion (light, Nesbitt, Light, & White, 2000), and limit students’ participation and voice (Zhao& McDougall, 2005). One possible strategy to circumvent these concerns is to use students or peers to facilitate thE discussion. Alimited number of researchers have begun to examine the use of peer-facilitation (e.g., Correia & Davis, 2007; Hewet al., 2010; Rourke & Anderson, 2002). The findings of these studies have found that students generally feel more comfortable to vocalise their views, brainstorm ideas,and challenge each other’s ideas in a peer-facilitated discussion environment. texts.full_text: customers of the problem and doing their best to 'contain' the situation.Of course I say this with a huge grain of salt. For one, I don't drive a Toyota model (I drive a Pontiac), so I can't even imagine what it must be like for those directly affected by these recalls. However, as someone involved in automotive repair and management through my work at SSGM Magazine, the situation raises several important points worth discussing. First is customer trust: how companies handle such crises significantly impacts their reputation with consumers. Toyota's proactive approach to notifying customers about potential issues demonstrates a commitment to safety over profit—a stance that should be commended. Secondly, this scenario highlights the importance of robust quality control measures within automotive manufacturing and repair industries alike. It serves as a reminder for all technicians working in garages across Canada—and indeed globally—to remain vigilant when it comes to vehicle inspections and maintenance routines. This recall underscores how even small issues can escalate into major safety concerns if not addressed promptly. For independent shops, this situation also presents an opportunity: ensuring that your services are reliable and trustworthy is paramount. By providing transparent communication with customers about any potential recalls or known defects in their vehicles—whether it's a Toyota model or otherwise—you reinforce the value of choosing local expertise over larger chains who may prioritize volume sales above customer service. Lastly, from a technical perspective, understanding how these issues arose can help technicians better diagnose and prevent similar problems. Autoload Database Firstly, ensure that your `database` library is set up for autoloading in CodeIgniter by adding `'db' => TRUE` under `$autoload['libraries']` within the `application/config/autoload.php`. ```php $autoload['libraries'] = array('database'); ``` ### 2. Custom Database Connection Check You can implement a custom check to see if your database connection is successful and handle errors accordingly. #### Using Hooks: 1. Enable hooks in CodeIgniter by setting `$config['enable_hooks']` to `TRUE` in the `application/config/config.php`. ```php $config['enable_hooks'] = TRUE; ``` 2. Create a hook that checks for database connection before any controller is loaded. 3. In your `application/hooks`, create or edit `pre_controller_hook.php`. texts.full_text: nd useful stuff Search for: Tag Archives: 2010 Happy New Year! 01 Jan 10the meta2010, beer, beerdrinker, blog, new year, resolutionrick It’s a new year and I have a lot of resolutions on my mind, and I’m working through what I want to really focus on. But two really stick in my mind as relevant and important. The first is to continue on the path that I set out on mid-year last year, cultivating relationships and habits that really help me move forward. The second is to make and drink more of my own beer, and specialty beers, and to spend less time drinking commodity beers, even from the great microbreweries. These are the adventures that I will be sharing over the course of this new year in this space, I hope that you will join me! cheers, rick Leave a comment notes from the other sideBret on About beerdrinker.orgm8ryx on Lompoc and their wonderful holiday funJohn Coates on Lompoc and their wonderful holiday fun --- Leave a comment Bret on About beerdrinker.org > Just found your site and I'm really enjoying your perspective on beer and life. It doesn’t matter at all!! Sometimes we just need to let things slide a little bit, and focus on our family (or other important people in your life). He'll be walking soon, and then running around like his sister did before him...and all of a sudden he’ll be grown-up enough for me not want anymore photos taken because they might embarrass us both when we look back on them later; but right now I need those memories captured while they're still little. But not quilting…that’s not swappable. See? And since I’m on the topic of “life” I’m going to add…thank you for visiting this blog. Thank you

Vidyamala’s personal experience of using Mindfulness to manage a debilitating health condition underpins the writing, and her wise, reassuring, clear-thinking voice rings through loud and clear. I found the many case studies of people finding ways to live well with their pain particularly reassuring. It gave me hope that it was possible to live WITH my body as it was, rather than always fighting against it or wishing my experience was different. texts.full_text: This has moved to https://github.com/confluen/conflue/wiki/Confluence-API#rest-api-requests-and-responses. The Confluence API provides a RESTful interface to interact with Confluence content. It allows you to create, read, update, and delete content, as well as perform various other operations. The API endpoint URLs are structured as follows: `https://your-confluence-domain.com/rest/api/content/{content-id}` Where `{content-id}` is the unique identifier of the content you want to interact with. The API supports various operations such as: - Getting content: Retrieve the details of a specific content. -

In the controller? While finding the issue is not “Where’s Waldo”-tier, our test is providing us with some pretty weak feedback. Given that this is an integration test, the problem is that we’ve most likely wired something up incorrectly. If we check our test.log, hidden in between the request and our database logs is the culprit: As we are oft to do, we forgot to whitelist the :title parameter, and so the parameter is being completely ignored: def foo_params params.require(:foo).permit(:first_name) Ideally our test would tell us that we’ve made a mistake wiring up our code, rather than the ambiguous error we’re getting. We can make our tests give us better feedback by reconfiguring Rails to throw an error when given unauthorized attributes. texts.full_text: nt invention generally relates to play-acting equipment and more particularly to a kit for realistically enacting the tooth fairy fable. 2. Prior Art Various types of kits and items have been provided for carrying out plays, games and other types of childhood amusements. One of the most endearing of all fables believed by very young children is the fable of the tooth fairy. The fable says that if a child's tooth is left for the tooth fairy, the tooth fairy will come at night after the child is asleep and exchange the tooth for a coin. In some versions of the fable, the tooth is left but a coin is also left in compensation for the child's loss of the tooth, sometimes a traumatic experience for the child with considerable pain and soreness in the gum area where the tooth has come out. Children, especially very young children, have come to rely on the tooth fairy fable, because of their inherent fascination with beautiful fairies and the world of make-believe, and also because of the monetary compensation received by the child. Parents have found that this fable is a convenient way of distracting the child from the pain and soreness and the fright encountered when a baby tooth is lost by the child. Obviously, the more realism that can be instilled when the tooth fairy fable iis enacted, the more enjoyable and believable the experience will be for the child. Unfortunately, many prior art kits and items have been somewhat lacking in realism, and parents have had to rely on their own imagination and creativity to make the tooth fairy fable come alive for their children. If it worked for Bollywood, it could work for Hollywood." Gandhigiri-style protests Since the release of the film, there have been instances where peaceful protests were either dubbed "Gandhigiri" or the protesters have claimed that they have been inspired by the movie. In the United States during July 2007, piles of flowers were sent to the United States Citizenship and Immigration Services office by individuals who were legally in the U.S. but caught in a green card backlog. This was an act of Gandhigiri (or nonviolent protest) copied from Lage Raho Munna Bhai. There have been positive reactions to this event. The USCIS shipped the flowers to the Walter Reed Army Medical Center and Bethesda Naval hospitals. A 15 July 2007 editorial in The Baltimore Sun argued, "let's hope their protest gets results - because Americans are fortunate to have living among them people who are this ingenious, and this thoughtful of others, and this capable of channeling anger into beauty."
